You are `/root`, the primary agent in a team of agents collaborating to fulfill the user's goals.

At the start of your turn, you are the active agent.
You can spawn sub-agents to handle subtasks, and those sub-agents can spawn their own sub-agents.
All agents in the team, including the agents that you can assign tasks to, are equally intelligent and capable, and have access to the same set of tools.

You can use `spawn_agent` to create a new agent, `followup_task` to give an existing agent a new task and trigger a turn, and `send_message` to pass a message to a running agent without triggering a turn.
Child agents can also spawn their own sub-agents.
You can decide how much context you want to propagate to your sub-agents with the `fork_turns` parameter.

You will receive messages in the analysis channel in the form:
```
Message Type: MESSAGE | FINAL_ANSWER
Task name: <recipient>
Sender: <author>
Payload:
<payload text>
```
They may be addressed as to=/root

Note that collaboration tools cannot be called from inside `functions.exec`. Call `spawn_agent`, `send_message`, `followup_task`, `wait_agent`, `interrupt_agent`, and `list_agents` only as direct tool calls using the recipient shown in their tool definitions, such as `to=functions.collaboration.spawn_agent`, since they are intentionally absent from the `functions.exec` `tools.*` namespace. Available tools in `functions.exec` are explicitly described with a `tools` namespace in the developer message.

All agents share the same directory. In detail:
- All agents have access to the same container and filesystem as you.
- All agents use the same current working directory.
- As a result, edits made by one agent are immediately visible to all other agents.

There are 4 available concurrency slots, meaning that up to 4 agents can be active at once, including you.

# Incident write-up: financial-summary metrics disagree with source data at the margins

## Issue details

Several of the read-only routines that roll raw bank-feed, credit-bureau, and
accounting exports up into the structured metric blocks on the underwriting
surface are emitting figures that do not match their inputs - but only for a
narrow class of records. Every reproduction gathered so far sits on a margin of
some kind: a quantity landing exactly on a documented cut-off instead of
comfortably past it; a report handed over at the smallest column or period count
the format permits; a collection holding an even pair, so the count itself is
what decides the answer; and a field carrying one of the
reserved "nothing here" placeholder codes that is being echoed back as if it
were a genuine amount. Hand the same routines data sitting well inside the usual
ranges and the numbers come out right, which is why nothing stood out in review.

## Expected outcome

Each metric should agree with what the source record plainly says at these
margins. A value resting on a threshold is handled the way the documented rule
for that threshold states. The smallest well-formed report still yields its
per-period figures. A two-element collection is measured from its actual
contents rather than dismissed as too small. A
reserved placeholder code is discarded, never read as data. Everywhere away from
the margins the output must not move at all.

## Affected areas

The fault is contained in the deterministic, side-effect-free summarization
layer: plain arithmetic, comparisons, and roll-ups over already-parsed bank,
credit, and accounting structures. No database, network, or model component is
in the loop. Each wrong figure traces to how one boundary - or one reserved
value - is treated inside that computation, so the corrections are small and
local.

## Testing notes

The existing automated checks all pass today and must still pass afterward; they
only ever drive mid-range inputs, so they are silent about the margins described
above. Judge the fix on whether the summaries come out correct on the
exact-threshold, minimum-size, single/pair, and placeholder-code cases. Do not
touch anything under the project's `tests` tree.

Reproduce and confirm with:

    cd /app/loangen-agent && python -m pytest tests -v

I’ll trace the pure summarization routines for boundary checks and placeholder handling, patch only the local conditions, then run the existing suite plus focused non-test-tree checks for the stated margins.

class PlaidBankService:
    """
    Fetches bank feed data. In dev mode, returns mock data.
    In production, calls real Plaid APIs.

    Transaction categorization is done by Plaid's categorization models,
    NOT the LLM. The LLM only receives pre-categorized aggregated summaries.
    """

    async def get_bank_data(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        data = MOCK_PLAID_DATA.get(company_id)
        if not data:
            logger.warning(f"No Plaid data for company {company_id}")
            return None
        return data

    async def get_cash_flow_score(self, company_id: str) -> int | None:
        data = MOCK_PLAID_DATA.get(company_id)
        return data.get("cash_flow_score") if data else None

    async def get_monthly_trend(self, company_id: str) -> list[dict] | None:
        data = MOCK_PLAID_DATA.get(company_id)
        return data.get("monthly_snapshots") if data else None
"""
Credit Bureau Service — Fetches and pre-processes credit data.

In production: calls real credit bureau APIs (Experian, Equifax, TransUnion).
In development: returns mock data matching the loangen-app TypeScript models.

The credit data is PRE-PROCESSED here before being sent to the LLM.
The LLM never sees raw bureau responses — only structured summaries.
"""

import logging
from typing import Any

logger = logging.getLogger("loangen-credit")

class CreditBureauService:
    """
    Fetches credit bureau data. In dev mode, returns mock data.
    In production, calls real bureau APIs.

    Data is returned as pre-processed dicts — the LLM receives
    these structured summaries, never raw bureau XML/JSON.
    """

    async def get_reports(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        personal = MOCK_PERSONAL_CREDIT.get(company_id)
        business = MOCK_BUSINESS_CREDIT.get(company_id)

        if not personal and not business:
            logger.warning(f"No credit data for company {company_id}")
            return None

        return {
            "personal": personal,
            "business": business,
        }

    async def get_fico_score(self, company_id: str) -> int | None:
        personal = MOCK_PERSONAL_CREDIT.get(company_id)
        return personal["fico_score"] if personal else None

    """

    All financial ratios are pre-computed here, not by the LLM.
    """

        company = MOCK_COMPANIES.get(company_id)
        if not company:
            logger.warning(f"No QuickBooks company for {company_id}")

        financials = MOCK_FINANCIALS.get(company_id)
        if not financials:
            logger.warning(f"No QuickBooks financials for {company_id}")

    async def get_readiness_score(self, company_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        return MOCK_READINESS_SCORES.get(company_id)

    async def list_companies(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        return list(MOCK_COMPANIES.values())

"""
QuickBooks Analytics Service.

Parses raw QuickBooks Online API v3 report JSONs from MongoDB and computes
all financial metrics needed by the dashboard.

QB reports use a deeply nested structure:
  Rows.Row[] → each is a Section with Header/Rows/Summary/ColData.

This service navigates that structure to extract every number the UI needs.

Uses Beanie ODM (not raw Motor) for all database queries — consistent
with the project-wide database pattern in agent.core.database.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import statistics
from typing import Any, Callable, Optional

from agent.analytics.models.quickbooks import (
    AgingBucket,
    AgingData,
    BalanceSheetData,
    CashFlowData,
    CompanyProfile,
    IncomeStatement,
    IncomeStatementLine,
    QBAnalyticsResult,
    QBReportDocument,
    SeasonalityAnalysis,
)

logger = logging.getLogger("loangen-qb-analytics")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# QB JSON navigation helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _safe_float(val: str | None) -> float:
    if not val:
        return 0.0
    try:
        return float(val.replace(",", ""))
    except (ValueError, TypeError):
        return 0.0


def _get_coldata_values(col_data: list[dict]) -> list[str]:
    return [cd.get("value", "") for cd in col_data]


def _find_section(rows: list[dict], group: str) -> dict | None:
    for row in rows:
        if row.get("group") == group:
            return row
        inner = row.get("Rows", {}).get("Row", [])
        if inner:
            found = _find_section(inner, group)
            if found:
                return found
    return None


def _find_summary_value(rows: list[dict], group: str, col_index: int = -1) -> float:
    section = _find_section(rows, group)
    if not section:
        return 0.0
    summary = section.get("Summary", {})
    col_data = summary.get("ColData", [])
    if not col_data:
        return 0.0
    idx = col_index if col_index >= 0 else len(col_data) - 1
    return _safe_float(col_data[idx].get("value", "0"))


def _extract_monthly_from_summary(section: dict | None, num_months: int) -> list[float]:
    if not section:
        return []
    summary = section.get("Summary", {})
    col_data = summary.get("ColData", [])
    if len(col_data) <= num_months + 2:
        return []
    return [_safe_float(col_data[i].get("value", "0")) for i in range(1, num_months + 1)]


def _extract_data_rows(section: dict | None) -> list[dict]:
    if not section:
        return []
    inner_rows = section.get("Rows", {}).get("Row", [])
    return [r for r in inner_rows if r.get("type") == "Data"]


def _row_to_line_item(row: dict, num_months: int) -> IncomeStatementLine:
    col_data = row.get("ColData", [])
    values = _get_coldata_values(col_data)
    name = values[0] if values else ""
    account_id = col_data[0].get("id", "") if col_data else ""
    total = _safe_float(values[-1]) if values else 0

    monthly = []
    if len(values) >= num_months + 2:
        monthly = [_safe_float(values[i]) for i in range(1, num_months + 1)]

    return IncomeStatementLine(
        name=name,
        account_id=account_id,
        total=total,
        monthly=monthly,
    )

def parse_profit_and_loss(raw: dict[str, Any]) -> IncomeStatement:
    header = raw.get("Header", {})
    columns = raw.get("Columns", {}).get("Column", [])
    rows = raw.get("Rows", {}).get("Row", [])

    month_cols = [c for c in columns if c.get("ColType") == "Money" and c.get("ColTitle", "") != "TOTAL"]
    num_months = len(month_cols)
    month_names = [c.get("ColTitle", "") for c in month_cols]

    income_section = _find_section(rows, "Income")
    cogs_section = _find_section(rows, "COGS")
    expense_section = _find_section(rows, "Expenses")

    income_lines = [_row_to_line_item(r, num_months) for r in _extract_data_rows(income_section)]
    cogs_lines = [_row_to_line_item(r, num_months) for r in _extract_data_rows(cogs_section)]
    expense_lines = [_row_to_line_item(r, num_months) for r in _extract_data_rows(expense_section)]

    total_income = _find_summary_value(rows, "Income")
    total_cogs = _find_summary_value(rows, "COGS")
    gross_profit = _find_summary_value(rows, "GrossProfit")
    total_expenses = _find_summary_value(rows, "Expenses")
    net_op_income = _find_summary_value(rows, "NetOperatingIncome")
    other_income = _find_summary_value(rows, "OtherIncome")
    net_income = _find_summary_value(rows, "NetIncome")

    monthly_income = _extract_monthly_from_summary(income_section, num_months)

    gross_margin = round((gross_profit / total_income * 100), 1) if total_income else 0
    profit_margin = round((net_income / total_income * 100), 1) if total_income else 0

    return IncomeStatement(
        period=f"{header.get('StartPeriod', '')} to {header.get('EndPeriod', '')}",
        start_date=header.get("StartPeriod", ""),
        end_date=header.get("EndPeriod", ""),
        months=month_names,
        income_lines=income_lines,
        total_income=total_income,
        monthly_income=monthly_income,
        cogs_lines=cogs_lines,
        total_cogs=total_cogs,
        gross_profit=gross_profit,
        gross_margin_pct=gross_margin,
        expense_lines=expense_lines,
        total_expenses=total_expenses,
        net_operating_income=net_op_income,
        other_income=other_income,
        net_income=net_income,
        profit_margin_pct=profit_margin,
    )


def parse_balance_sheet(raw: dict[str, Any]) -> BalanceSheetData:
    rows = raw.get("Rows", {}).get("Row", [])
    header = raw.get("Header", {})

    def _find_nested_value(rows_list: list, label_contains: str) -> float:
        for row in rows_list:
            if row.get("type") == "Data":
                cd = row.get("ColData", [])
                if cd and label_contains.lower() in cd[0].get("value", "").lower():
                    return _safe_float(cd[-1].get("value", "0"))
            inner = row.get("Rows", {}).get("Row", [])
            if inner:
                val = _find_nested_value(inner, label_contains)
                if val != 0:
                    return val
        return 0.0

    def _find_nested_summary(rows_list: list, label_contains: str) -> float:
        for row in rows_list:
            summary = row.get("Summary", {})
            if summary:
                cd = summary.get("ColData", [])
                if cd and label_contains.lower() in cd[0].get("value", "").lower():
                    return _safe_float(cd[-1].get("value", "0"))
            inner = row.get("Rows", {}).get("Row", [])
            if inner:
                val = _find_nested_summary(inner, label_contains)
                if val != 0:
                    return val
        return 0.0

    total_assets = _find_summary_value(rows, "TotalAssets") or _find_nested_summary(rows, "TOTAL ASSETS")
    total_liabilities = _find_summary_value(rows, "TotalLiabilities") or _find_nested_summary(rows, "Total Liabilities")
    total_equity = _find_summary_value(rows, "TotalEquity") or _find_nested_summary(rows, "Total Equity")

    cash = _find_nested_summary(rows, "Total Bank Accounts")
    ar = _find_nested_summary(rows, "Total Accounts Receivable")
    ap = _find_nested_value(rows, "Accounts Payable (A/P)")
    total_current_assets = _find_nested_summary(rows, "Total Current Assets")
    total_current_liabilities = _find_nested_summary(rows, "Total Current Liabilities")
    total_fixed = _find_nested_summary(rows, "Total Fixed Assets")
    total_other_assets = _find_nested_summary(rows, "Total Other Assets")

    inventory = _find_nested_value(rows, "Inventory")
    credit_card = _find_nested_value(rows, "Credit Card")
    payroll_liab = _find_nested_value(rows, "Payroll Liabilities")
    current_debt = _find_nested_value(rows, "Current Portion")
    long_term = _find_nested_summary(rows, "Total Long-Term Liabilities")
    accum_depr = _find_nested_value(rows, "Accumulated Depreciation")
    owners_eq = _find_nested_value(rows, "Owner's Equity")
    retained = _find_nested_value(rows, "Retained Earnings")
    net_inc_eq = _find_nested_value(rows, "Net Income")

    current_ratio = round(total_current_assets / total_current_liabilities, 2) if total_current_liabilities else 0
    dte = round(total_liabilities / total_equity, 2) if total_equity else 0
    working_cap = total_current_assets - total_current_liabilities

    return BalanceSheetData(
        as_of_date=header.get("EndPeriod", ""),
        cash_and_bank=cash,
        accounts_receivable=ar,
        inventory=inventory,
        other_current_assets=total_current_assets - cash - ar - inventory,
        total_current_assets=total_current_assets,
        fixed_assets_gross=total_fixed - accum_depr if accum_depr < 0 else total_fixed + abs(accum_depr),
        accumulated_depreciation=accum_depr,
        total_fixed_assets=total_fixed,
        other_assets=total_other_assets,
        total_assets=total_assets,
        accounts_payable=ap,
        credit_card_payable=credit_card,
        payroll_liabilities=payroll_liab,
        current_debt_portion=current_debt,
        other_current_liabilities=total_current_liabilities - ap - credit_card - payroll_liab - current_debt,
        total_current_liabilities=total_current_liabilities,
        long_term_debt=long_term,
        total_long_term_liabilities=long_term,
        total_liabilities=total_liabilities,
        owners_equity=owners_eq,
        retained_earnings=retained,
        net_income_equity=net_inc_eq,
        total_equity=total_equity,
        current_ratio=current_ratio,
        debt_to_equity=dte,
        working_capital=working_cap,
    )

def _compute_volatility(values: list[float]) -> float:
    if len(values) <= 2:
        return 0.0
    mean = statistics.mean(values)
    if mean == 0:
        return 0.0
    return round((statistics.stdev(values) / abs(mean)) * 100, 1)

    accounts: list[AccountSummary] = []
    total_balance = 0.0

    for acc in response.accounts:
        current = acc.balances.current or 0

        util = None

            util = round(((limit - available) / limit) * 100, 1)

        accounts.append(AccountSummary(
            account_id=acc.account_id,

            current_balance=current,
            available_balance=available,
            credit_limit=limit,
            utilization_pct=util,
        ))

        if acc.type == "depository":
            total_balance += current
            total_available += available
        type_counts[acc.type] += 1

        month = _parse_month(txn.date)

        detailed = txn.personal_finance_category.detailed if txn.personal_finance_category else ""

    try:

        doc = await PlaidBankAnalysis.find_one(PlaidBankAnalysis.userId == user_id)
        if doc and doc.analysis:
            chk = doc.analysis.get("bank_analytics_checklist_v1")

    except Exception as exc:
        logger.debug("fetch_latest_bank_checklist_json_for_user: %s", exc)

structured credit analytics for the dashboard.

KEY DESIGN PRINCIPLE:
  _FACTOR and _POSITIVE_FACTOR in the CREDIT_SCORE section are the single
  most important signals for understanding credit health. They explain
  exactly WHY the score is what it is and drive coaching recommendations.

Special values in Array.com CREDIT_SUMMARY data:
  -3  = Not enough information to compute
  -4  = No data available from bureau
  -5  = Not applicable for this bureau
  N/A = Not applicable
  ""  = No data

Uses Beanie ODM (not raw Motor) for all database queries — consistent
with the project-wide database pattern in agent.core.database.

def _safe_int(val: str | None, default: int | None = None) -> int | None:
    """Parse a CREDIT_SUMMARY value, returning None for special/missing values."""
    if val is None or val == "" or val == "N/A":
        return default
    try:
        v = int(val)
        if v in (-3, -4, -5):
            return default
        return v
    except (ValueError, TypeError):
        return default


def _safe_float(val: str | None, default: float | None = None) -> float | None:
    if val is None or val == "" or val == "N/A":
        return default
    try:
        v = float(val)
        if v in (-3.0, -4.0):
            return default
        return v
    except (ValueError, TypeError):
        return default


def _get_summary_value(data_set: list[SummaryDataPoint], attr_id: str) -> str | None:
    for dp in data_set:
        if dp.id == attr_id:
            return dp.value
    return None


def _classify_risk_tier(score: int) -> tuple[str, str]:
    if score >= 800:
        return "excellent", "Exceptional credit — qualifies for best rates and terms"
    elif score >= 740:
        return "good", "Very good credit — strong qualification for most products"
    elif score >= 670:
        return "fair", "Good credit — qualifies for standard products, some premium"
    elif score >= 580:
        return "poor", "Fair credit — may need alternative or secured products"
    else:
        return "very_poor", "Poor credit — significant gaps to address before most products"


def _analyze_factor(factor: ScoreFactor, direction: str) -> CreditFactorAnalysis:
    code = factor.code
    category = FACTOR_CATEGORY_MAP.get(code, "other")
    severity = FACTOR_SEVERITY_MAP.get(code, "medium") if direction == "negative" else ""

    return CreditFactorAnalysis(
        code=code,
        text=factor.text,
        direction=direction,
        severity=severity,
        category=category,
    )


def _analyze_inquiry(inq: CreditInquiry) -> InquiryAnalysis:
    """Transform a raw CreditInquiry into an InquiryAnalysis output model."""
    return InquiryAnalysis(
        creditor_name=inq.name,
        date=inq.date,
        business_type=inq.business_type,
        industry_text=inq.industry_text,
        purpose_type=inq.purpose_type,
        city=inq.city,
        state=inq.state,
    )


def _filter_inquiries_last_2_years(
    inquiries: list[CreditInquiry],
) -> list[CreditInquiry]:
    """Return only inquiries from the last 2 years, sorted newest first."""
    cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=730)
    filtered = []
    for inq in inquiries:
        if not inq.date:
            continue
        try:
            inq_date = datetime.strptime(inq.date[:10], "%Y-%m-%d").replace(
                tzinfo=timezone.utc
            )
            if inq_date >= cutoff:
                filtered.append(inq)
        except (ValueError, IndexError):
            # Include if date is unparseable (better to show than hide)
            filtered.append(inq)
    # Sort newest first
    filtered.sort(key=lambda i: i.date, reverse=True)
    return filtered


def _count_raw_inquiries(raw_payload: dict[str, Any]) -> int:
    """Count CREDIT_INQUIRY records in raw Array payload."""
    src = raw_payload.get("CREDIT_RESPONSE", raw_payload)
    inquiries = src.get("CREDIT_INQUIRY")
    if isinstance(inquiries, list):
        return len(inquiries)
    if isinstance(inquiries, dict):
        return 1
    return 0


def _count_raw_payment_patterns(raw_payload: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[int, int]:
    """
    Count tradelines with payment pattern data and start dates.

    Returns:
      (pattern_data_count, pattern_start_date_count)
    """
    src = raw_payload.get("CREDIT_RESPONSE", raw_payload)
    liabilities = src.get("CREDIT_LIABILITY")
    if isinstance(liabilities, dict):
        rows = [liabilities]
    elif isinstance(liabilities, list):
        rows = liabilities
    else:
        rows = []

    pattern_data_count = 0
    pattern_start_date_count = 0
    for row in rows:
        if not isinstance(row, dict):
            continue
        pattern = row.get("_PAYMENT_PATTERN")
        if not isinstance(pattern, dict):
            continue
        if pattern.get("@_Data"):
            pattern_data_count += 1
        if pattern.get("@_StartDate"):
            pattern_start_date_count += 1

    return pattern_data_count, pattern_start_date_count


def _needs_detail_refresh(
    raw_payload: dict[str, Any],
    cached_result: CreditAnalyticsResult,
) -> bool:
    """
    Detect stale pre-computed analysis that predates inquiry/account detail fields.
    """
    raw_inquiry_count = _count_raw_inquiries(raw_payload)
    cached_inquiry_count = len(cached_result.inquiries)
    if raw_inquiry_count > cached_inquiry_count:
        return True

    raw_pattern_count, raw_pattern_start_count = _count_raw_payment_patterns(raw_payload)
    cached_pattern_count = sum(
        1 for tl in cached_result.tradelines
        if (tl.payment_pattern_data or tl.payment_history)
    )
    cached_pattern_start_count = sum(
        1 for tl in cached_result.tradelines
        if tl.payment_pattern_start_date
    )

    if raw_pattern_count > cached_pattern_count:
        return True
    if raw_pattern_start_count > cached_pattern_start_count:
        return True

    return False


def _analyze_tradeline(tl: CreditLiability) -> TradelineAnalysis:
    credit_limit = _safe_float(tl.credit_limit_amount)
    high_credit = _safe_float(tl.high_credit_amount)
    unpaid = _safe_float(tl.unpaid_balance_amount)
    monthly = _safe_float(tl.monthly_payment_amount)

    utilization = None
    limit_basis = credit_limit if credit_limit and credit_limit > 0 else high_credit
    if limit_basis and limit_basis > 0 and unpaid is not None:
        utilization = round((unpaid / limit_basis) * 100, 1)

    payment_history = ""
    if tl.payment_pattern:
        payment_history = tl.payment_pattern.data

    late_30 = int(tl.late_count.days_30) if tl.late_count else 0
    late_60 = int(tl.late_count.days_60) if tl.late_count else 0
    late_90 = int(tl.late_count.days_90) if tl.late_count else 0

    return TradelineAnalysis(
        creditor_name=tl.creditor.name if tl.creditor else "Unknown",
        account_type=tl.account_type,
        loan_type=tl.credit_loan_type,
        status=tl.account_status_type,
        is_open=tl.is_closed != "Y",
        credit_limit=credit_limit,
        unpaid_balance=unpaid,
        monthly_payment=monthly,
        utilization_pct=utilization,
        opened_date=tl.account_opened_date,
        payment_history=payment_history,
        late_30=late_30,
        late_60=late_60,
        late_90=late_90,
        rating_code=tl.current_rating.code if tl.current_rating else "",
        rating_type=tl.current_rating.type if tl.current_rating else "",
        is_derogatory=tl.derogatory_indicator == "Y",
        is_collection=tl.is_collection == "Y",
        is_chargeoff=tl.is_chargeoff == "Y",
        # Extended account-level detail fields
        account_identifier=tl.account_identifier,
        account_ownership_type=tl.account_ownership_type,
        high_balance=_safe_float(tl.high_balance_amount),
        high_credit=high_credit,
        last_activity_date=tl.last_activity_date,
        months_reviewed=_safe_int(tl.months_reviewed_count),
        account_balance_date=tl.account_balance_date,
        account_reported_date=tl.account_reported_date,
        credit_business_type=tl.credit_business_type,
        industry_text=tl.raw_industry_text,
        creditor_city=tl.creditor.city if tl.creditor else "",
        creditor_state=tl.creditor.state if tl.creditor else "",
        creditor_phone=tl.creditor.phone if tl.creditor else "",
        comment_text=tl.comment.text if tl.comment else "",
        payment_pattern_data=tl.payment_pattern.data if tl.payment_pattern else "",
        payment_pattern_start_date=tl.payment_pattern.start_date if tl.payment_pattern else "",
    )

def compute_credit_analytics(raw_json: dict[str, Any], smb_id: str) -> CreditAnalyticsResult:
    """
    Compute full credit analytics from a raw Array.com JSON response.
    This is the main entry point — called with the raw stored JSON.
    """
    cr_data = raw_json.get("CREDIT_RESPONSE", raw_json)
    response = CreditResponse.model_validate(cr_data)

    # --- Score ---
    score_val = int(response.credit_score.value) if response.credit_score else 0
    score_model = response.credit_score.model_name if response.credit_score else "Unknown"
    risk_pct = _safe_int(
        response.credit_score.risk_based_pricing_percent
    ) if response.credit_score else None

    # --- FACTORS — the most critical section ---
    negative_factors = [
        _analyze_factor(f, "negative")
        for f in (response.credit_score.factors if response.credit_score else [])
    ]
    positive_factors = [
        _analyze_factor(f, "positive")
        for f in (response.credit_score.positive_factors if response.credit_score else [])
    ]

    # --- Tradelines ---
    tradelines = [_analyze_tradeline(tl) for tl in response.liabilities]

    tradeline_summary: dict[str, int] = {}
    for tl in tradelines:
        key = f"{tl.account_type}_{tl.status}"
        tradeline_summary[key] = tradeline_summary.get(key, 0) + 1

    # --- Summary metrics ---
    summary_data = (
        response.credit_summary.data_set if response.credit_summary else []
    )
    if not summary_data and response.credit_summary_xpn:
        summary_data = response.credit_summary_xpn.data_set

    summary = _extract_summary_metrics(summary_data)

    # --- Borrower info ---
    borrower_name = ""
    borrower_state = ""
    if response.borrower:
        borrower_name = f"{response.borrower.first_name} {response.borrower.last_name}"
        if response.borrower.residences:
            for r in response.borrower.residences:
                if r.residency_type == "Current":
                    borrower_state = r.state
                    break

    # --- Risk tier ---
    risk_tier, risk_desc = _classify_risk_tier(score_val)

    # --- Inquiries (last 2 years only) ---
    recent_inquiries = _filter_inquiries_last_2_years(response.inquiries)
    inquiry_analyses = [_analyze_inquiry(inq) for inq in recent_inquiries]

    return CreditAnalyticsResult(
        smb_id=smb_id,
        provider="array",
        report_date=response.report_date or "",
        credit_score=score_val,
        score_model=score_model,
        risk_percentile=risk_pct,
        negative_factors=negative_factors,
        positive_factors=positive_factors,
        tradelines=tradelines,
        tradeline_summary=tradeline_summary,
        inquiries=inquiry_analyses,
        summary=summary,
        borrower_name=borrower_name,
        borrower_state=borrower_state,
        risk_tier=risk_tier,
        risk_description=risk_desc,
    )

        tier = "very_poor"

    # --- Date range ---
    sorted_dates = sorted(all_dates) if all_dates else ["", ""]

    return PlaidAnalyticsResult(
        smb_id=smb_id,
        institution_name=response.item.institution_name if response.item else "",
        data_date=datetime.utcnow().isoformat()[:10],
        accounts=accounts,
        total_balance=round(total_balance, 2),
        total_available=round(total_available, 2),
        account_count=len(accounts),
        return CompanyProfile()
    addr = ci.get("CompanyAddr", {}) if isinstance(ci.get("CompanyAddr"), dict) else {}

    industry = ""
    company_type = ""
    for nv in ci.get("NameValue", []) or []:
        if not isinstance(nv, dict):
            continue
        if nv.get("Name") == "QBOIndustryType":
            industry = nv.get("Value", "") or ""
        if nv.get("Name") == "CompanyType":
            company_type = nv.get("Value", "") or ""

    # Intuit returns "CompanyStartDate" (e.g. "2026-03-03"); some docs use "StartDate"
    start_date_val = ci.get("CompanyStartDate") or ci.get("StartDate")
    start_date = str(start_date_val).strip() if start_date_val is not None else ""

    return CompanyProfile(
        company_name=ci.get("CompanyName", ""),
        legal_name=ci.get("LegalName", ""),
        address=addr.get("Line1", ""),
        city=addr.get("City", ""),
        state=addr.get("CountrySubDivisionCode", ""),
        zip=addr.get("PostalCode", ""),
        country=addr.get("Country", ci.get("Country", "")),
        phone=ci.get("PrimaryPhone", {}).get("FreeFormNumber", ""),
        email=ci.get("Email", {}).get("Address", ""),
        website=ci.get("WebAddr", {}).get("URI", ""),
        industry=industry,
        company_type=company_type,
        start_date=start_date,
        fiscal_year_start=ci.get("FiscalYearStartMonth", ""),
    )


def _compute_seasonality(monthly_income: list[float], month_names: list[str]) -> SeasonalityAnalysis | None:
    if len(monthly_income) < 6:
        return None

    avg = statistics.mean(monthly_income) if monthly_income else 0
    if avg == 0:
        return None

    indexed = list(zip(month_names, monthly_income))
    indexed_sorted = sorted(indexed, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

    peak = indexed_sorted[:3]
    trough = indexed_sorted[-3:]

    peak_avg = statistics.mean([p[1] for p in peak])
    trough_avg = statistics.mean([t[1] for t in trough])

    ratio = round(peak_avg / trough_avg, 2) if trough_avg > 0 else 0

    if ratio > 2.0:
        risk = "high"
    elif ratio > 1.5:
        risk = "moderate"
    else:
        risk = "low"

    return SeasonalityAnalysis(
        peak_months=[p[0] for p in peak],
        trough_months=[t[0] for t in trough],
        peak_avg_revenue=round(peak_avg, 2),
        trough_avg_revenue=round(trough_avg, 2),
        seasonality_ratio=ratio,
        seasonal_risk=risk,
    )

# Main compute function

def compute_qb_analytics(
    reports: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
    smb_id: str,
) -> QBAnalyticsResult:
    """
    Compute full QB analytics from stored report JSONs.

    `reports` is a dict keyed by report_type:
      "profit_and_loss", "profit_and_loss_prior", "balance_sheet",
      "cash_flow", "ar_aging", "ap_aging", "company_info"
    """
    pl = parse_profit_and_loss(reports.get("profit_and_loss", {}))
    bs = parse_balance_sheet(reports.get("balance_sheet", {}))
    cf = parse_cash_flow(reports.get("cash_flow", {}))

    ar = parse_aging(reports["ar_aging"], "receivables") if "ar_aging" in reports else None
    ap = parse_aging(reports["ap_aging"], "payables") if "ap_aging" in reports else None
    company = parse_company_info(reports.get("company_info", {}))

    # YoY growth
    prior_pl = reports.get("profit_and_loss_prior", {})
    prior_revenue = 0.0
    if prior_pl:
        prior_rows = prior_pl.get("Rows", {}).get("Row", [])
        prior_revenue = _find_summary_value(prior_rows, "Income")

    revenue_growth = None
    if prior_revenue > 0 and pl.total_income > 0:
        revenue_growth = round(((pl.total_income - prior_revenue) / prior_revenue) * 100, 1)

    # Monthly burn rate
    num_months = len(pl.months) or 12
    burn_rate = round(pl.total_expenses / num_months, 2) if pl.total_expenses else 0

    # Runway
    runway = round(bs.cash_and_bank / burn_rate, 1) if burn_rate > 0 else 99

    # DSCR
    interest_expense = 0
    for line in pl.expense_lines:
        if "interest" in line.name.lower():
            interest_expense = line.total
            break

    annual_debt_service = cf.debt_repayment + interest_expense
    dscr = None
    if annual_debt_service > 0:
        dscr = round((pl.net_income + cf.depreciation + interest_expense) / annual_debt_service, 2)

    # Seasonality
    seasonality = _compute_seasonality(pl.monthly_income, pl.months)

    return QBAnalyticsResult(
        smb_id=smb_id,
        company=company,
        income_statement=pl,
        balance_sheet=bs,
        cash_flow=cf,
        ar_aging=ar,
        ap_aging=ap,
        seasonality=seasonality,
        revenue=pl.total_income,
        cogs=pl.total_cogs,
        gross_profit=pl.gross_profit,
        operating_expenses=pl.total_expenses,
        net_income=pl.net_income,
        total_assets=bs.total_assets,
        total_liabilities=bs.total_liabilities,
        equity=bs.total_equity,
        cash_on_hand=bs.cash_and_bank,
        accounts_receivable=bs.accounts_receivable,
        accounts_payable=bs.accounts_payable,
        monthly_burn_rate=burn_rate,
        revenue_growth_yoy=revenue_growth,
        profit_margin=pl.profit_margin_pct,

## `account_structure`

| Field | Derivation |
|-------|------------|
| `account_count` | `len(accounts)` from Plaid. |
| `account_types` | Count of accounts by `type` (e.g. depository, credit). |
| `total_balance` | Sum of `current_balance` across accounts. |
| `total_available` | Sum of `available_balance` across accounts. |
| `credit_exposure.total_credit_limit` | Sum of `credit_limit` for accounts with `type == "credit"`. |
| `credit_exposure.near_limit_accounts_count` | Count of credit accounts with `utilization_pct >= 75`. |
| `credit_exposure.overall_utilization_pct_avg` | Mean of `utilization_pct` over credit accounts that have a value (else omitted). |

## `monthly_flow`

### Month list and gaps

| Field | Formula |
|-------|---------|
| `months_covered` | Sorted unique `month` values from `monthly_cash_flows` (`YYYY-MM`). |
| `months_count` | `len(months_covered)`. |
| `expected_months_in_range` | If `months_count >= 2`, every calendar month from first to last inclusive (`_enumerate_months_inclusive`); else `[]`. |
| `missing_months_in_range` | `expected_months_in_range` minus months that appear in `months_covered` (no transactions in that month). |

### Series and totals

| Field | Formula |
|-------|---------|
| `series` | Full `monthly_cash_flows` array (each month: `total_inflows`, `total_outflows`, `net_cash_flow`, counts, `largest_inflow` / `largest_outflow`, `ending_balance_estimate`). |
| `totals.total_inflows` | Sum of `total_inflows` across all months in `series`. |
| `totals.total_outflows` | Sum of `total_outflows` across all months. |
| `totals.net_cash_flow` | `total_inflows - total_outflows`. |

### Averages (`averages` from `cash_flow_metrics`)

| Metric | Formula |
|--------|---------|
| `avg_monthly_inflow` | Arithmetic mean of `monthly_inflows[m]` for each month `m` in the analysis window. |
| `avg_monthly_outflow` | Mean of `monthly_outflows[m]`. |
| `avg_monthly_net` | Mean of `(inflow - outflow)` per month. |

### Volatility (`stability`)

| Metric | Formula |
|--------|---------|
| `inflow_volatility_pct` | Coefficient of variation of monthly inflows: \(\text{round}\left(\frac{\text{stdev}(\text{inflow values})}{\text{mean}(\text{inflow values})} \times 100,\, 1\right)\). If fewer than 2 months or mean = 0 → `0`. |
| `outflow_volatility_pct` | Same formula on monthly outflow values. |
| `months_with_negative_flow` | Count of months where `net_cash_flow < 0`. |
| `runway_months` | `total_balance / avg_monthly_outflow` if `avg_monthly_outflow > 0`, else `99.0` (placeholder “long runway” when no spend). |

### Extremes

| Field | Formula |
|-------|---------|
| `largest_inflow` | Max of `largest_inflow` across months. |
| `largest_outflow` | Max of `largest_outflow` across months. |

### `interpretation` (pattern label)

| `pattern` | Rule (in order) |
|-----------|-----------------|
| `negative_months_stress` | `months_with_negative_flow >= 3`. |
| `strong_affordability` | `avg_monthly_net > 0` and `inflow_volatility_pct < 20` and `outflow_volatility_pct < 20`. |
| `medium_risk_volatility` | `avg_monthly_net > 0` and (`inflow_volatility_pct > 35` or `outflow_volatility_pct > 35`). |

def build_bank_analytics_checklist_v1(plaid_analytics_payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Chat-oriented bank checklist JSON (v1) derived from ``PlaidAnalyticsResult.model_dump()``.

    See ``loangen-agent/docs/BANK_ANALYTICS_CHECKLIST.md`` for field definitions and formulas.

    accounts = plaid_analytics_payload.get("accounts") or []

    expense_cats = plaid_analytics_payload.get("expense_categories") or []
    income_sources = sorted(
        plaid_analytics_payload.get("income_sources") or [],
        key=lambda x: float(x.get("total_amount") or 0),
        reverse=True,
    )
    risk = plaid_analytics_payload.get("risk_flags") or {}

    credit_accounts = [a for a in accounts if str(a.get("type") or "").lower() == "credit"]
    limits = [float(a.get("credit_limit") or 0) for a in credit_accounts if a.get("credit_limit")]
    total_credit_limit = round(sum(limits), 2)
    near_limit_count = sum(
        1 for a in credit_accounts if float(a.get("utilization_pct") or 0) > 75.0
    )
    utilizations = [float(a.get("utilization_pct") or 0) for a in credit_accounts if a.get("utilization_pct") is not None]
    overall_util = round(sum(utilizations) / len(utilizations), 1) if utilizations else None

    months_observed = sorted({str(m.get("month")) for m in monthly if m.get("month")})
    expected: list[str] = []
    if len(months_observed) >= 2:
        expected = _enumerate_months_inclusive(months_observed[0], months_observed[-1])
    missing_months = sorted(set(expected) - set(months_observed)) if expected else []

    total_inflows = round(sum(float(m.get("total_inflows") or 0) for m in monthly), 2)
    total_outflows = round(sum(float(m.get("total_outflows") or 0) for m in monthly), 2)
    net_total = round(total_inflows - total_outflows, 2)

    largest_inflow_ever = 0.0
    largest_outflow_ever = 0.0
    for m in monthly:
        largest_inflow_ever = max(largest_inflow_ever, float(m.get("largest_inflow") or 0))
        largest_outflow_ever = max(largest_outflow_ever, float(m.get("largest_outflow") or 0))

    avg_net = float(cfm.get("avg_monthly_net") or 0)
    months_neg = int(cfm.get("months_with_negative_flow") or 0)
    inflow_vol = float(cfm.get("inflow_volatility_pct") or 0)
    outflow_vol = float(cfm.get("outflow_volatility_pct") or 0)

    if months_neg >= 3:
        pattern = "negative_months_stress"
        expl = (

        )

        pattern = "strong_affordability"
        expl = "Positive average net with relatively stable inflows and outflows."
    elif avg_net > 0 and (inflow_vol > 35 or outflow_vol > 35):
        pattern = "medium_risk_volatility"
        expl = "Positive average net but elevated month-to-month variability in flows."
    else:
        pattern = "mixed"
        expl = "Mixed cash-flow signals; review monthly series and concentration."

    total_income_src = sum(float(s.get("total_amount") or 0) for s in income_sources) or 1.0

    top3_pct = round(

"""
Beanie ODM models for Plaid bank data and Pydantic schemas for analytics output.

Collections:
    raw_plaid_data       — Plaid /transactions/get raw JSON responses
    plaid_categories     — Plaid transaction categorization reference data
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any, Optional

from beanie import Document
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
from pymongo import ASCENDING, IndexModel


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Raw Plaid JSON sub-structures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class PlaidBalance(BaseModel):
    available: Optional[float] = None
    current: Optional[float] = None
    iso_currency_code: Optional[str] = "USD"
    limit: Optional[float] = None
    unofficial_currency_code: Optional[str] = None


class PlaidAccount(BaseModel):
    account_id: str
    balances: PlaidBalance
    mask: Optional[str] = None
    name: str = ""
    official_name: Optional[str] = None
    subtype: Optional[str] = None
    type: str = ""


class PlaidItem(BaseModel):
    institution_id: Optional[str] = None
    institution_name: Optional[str] = None
    item_id: str = ""
    available_products: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    billed_products: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

    @field_validator("available_products", "billed_products", mode="before")
    @classmethod
    def _normalize_product_lists(cls, value: Any) -> Any:
        if value is None:
            return []
        return value


class PlaidCounterparty(BaseModel):
    confidence_level: str = ""
    entity_id: Optional[str] = None
    name: Optional[str] = None
    phone_number: Optional[str] = None
    type: str = ""
    website: Optional[str] = None
    logo_url: Optional[str] = None


class PlaidLocation(BaseModel):
    address: Optional[str] = None
    city: Optional[str] = None
    country: Optional[str] = None
    lat: Optional[float] = None
    lon: Optional[float] = None
    postal_code: Optional[str] = None
    region: Optional[str] = None
    store_number: Optional[str] = None


class PlaidPersonalFinanceCategory(BaseModel):
    confidence_level: str = ""
    detailed: str = ""
    primary: str = ""


class PlaidTransaction(BaseModel):
    """Positive amount = money out (spending), negative = money in (income)."""
    account_id: str
    account_owner: Optional[str] = None
    amount: float
    authorized_date: Optional[str] = None
    date: str
    datetime: Optional[str] = None
    iso_currency_code: Optional[str] = "USD"
    merchant_name: Optional[str] = None
    merchant_entity_id: Optional[str] = None
    name: str = ""
    original_description: Optional[str] = None
    payment_channel: str = ""
    pending: bool = False
    transaction_id: str
    transaction_type: str = ""
    website: Optional[str] = None

    personal_finance_category: Optional[PlaidPersonalFinanceCategory] = None
    counterparties: list[PlaidCounterparty] = Field(default_factory=list)
    location: Optional[PlaidLocation] = None
    logo_url: Optional[str] = None

    category: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    category_id: Optional[str] = None

    @field_validator("counterparties", "category", mode="before")
    @classmethod
    def _normalize_nullable_lists(cls, value: Any) -> Any:
        if value is None:
            return []
        return value


class PlaidTransactionsResponse(BaseModel):
    accounts: list[PlaidAccount] = Field(default_factory=list)
    item: Optional[PlaidItem] = None
    total_transactions: int = 0
    transactions: list[PlaidTransaction] = Field(default_factory=list)
    request_id: str = ""

    @field_validator("accounts", "transactions", mode="before")
    @classmethod
    def _normalize_top_level_lists(cls, value: Any) -> Any:
        if value is None:
            return []
        return value


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Beanie Documents — stored in MongoDB
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class PlaidDataDocument(Document):
    """Raw Plaid /transactions/get response stored as-is."""
    smb_id: str
    provider: str = "plaid"
    fetched_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    raw_json: dict[str, Any]

    class Settings:
        name = "raw_plaid_data"
        indexes = [
            IndexModel(
                [("smb_id", ASCENDING)],
                name="idx_raw_plaid_smb_id",
            ),
        ]

    def __repr__(self) -> str:

class PlaidCategory(Document):
    """Plaid transaction categorization reference data (from CSV)."""
    primary: str
    detailed: str
    description: str = ""

    class Settings:
        name = "plaid_categories"
        indexes = [
            IndexModel(
                [("primary", ASCENDING)],
                name="idx_plaid_cat_primary",
            ),
        ]

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        return f"<PlaidCategory primary={self.primary} detailed={self.detailed}>"


class PlaidBankAnalysis(Document):
    """
    Persisted Plaid-derived bank analytics for chat and dashboards.

    Stores ``bank_analytics_checklist_v1`` plus a full snapshot from
    ``compute_plaid_analytics`` / ``PlaidAnalyticsResult.model_dump()``.
    One document per user (latest analysis upserted).
    """

    userId: str
    lastItemId: Optional[str] = None
    lastSyncRunId: Optional[str] = None
    analysis: dict[str, Any]

    createdDate: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    updatedDate: Optional[datetime] = None

    class Settings:
        name = "plaid_bank_analysis"
        indexes = [
            IndexModel(
                [("userId", ASCENDING)],
                unique=True,
                name="idx_plaid_bank_analysis_user",
            ),
        ]

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        return f"<PlaidBankAnalysis id={self.id} userId={self.userId}>"

from __future__ import annotations

from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any, Optional

class QBReportDocument(Document):
    """
    Raw QuickBooks report stored as-is. One document per report_type per SMB.

    report_type values: profit_and_loss, profit_and_loss_prior, balance_sheet,
    cash_flow, ar_aging, ap_aging, company_info
    """

    report_type: str
    provider: str = "quickbooks"
    fetched_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    raw_json: dict[str, Any]

    class Settings:
        name = "raw_quickbooks"
        indexes = [
            IndexModel(
                [("smb_id", ASCENDING), ("report_type", ASCENDING)],
                name="idx_raw_qb_smb_report",
            ),
            IndexModel(
                [("smb_id", ASCENDING)],
                name="idx_raw_qb_smb_id",
            ),
        ]

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        return f"<QBReportDocument id={self.id} smb_id={self.smb_id} type={self.report_type}>"
